title: Delete Saved Object Views
story: As a product administrator, I want to delete a saved Object View, so that I can remove views that are no longer needed and keep the list clean.
description:
Adds the ability to delete saved Object Views from the views panel. A 'Delete View' button appears below the object checkboxes when a saved view (not 'All') is selected.

Deletion behavior:
- Delete icon/button is only visible when a non-default view is selected (not available for the 'All' view).
- Clicking delete triggers a confirmation modal before proceeding.
- On confirmation, the view and all its associated saved objects are removed from the database.
- After deletion, the dropdown resets to 'All' and the delete button is hidden.

Permission restriction: The delete capability should not be available to users with the 'viewer' role. This maps to the shared-user permission system — invited users who only have view access cannot delete views.
acceptance_criteria: A 'Delete View' button appears below the checkboxes when a saved view is selected
Delete button is hidden when the 'All' view is selected
Clicking delete shows a confirmation modal
Confirming deletion removes the view and its associated objects from the database
After deletion, dropdown resets to 'All' and the view is removed from the dropdown list
Delete button is hidden for users with viewer-level permissions
Users with editor or admin permissions can delete views
ux_changes: Adds a delete button/icon in the views panel, visible only for non-default saved views. Confirmation modal on delete. Button hidden for viewer-role users.
middleware_changes: Server-side delete handler removes the view record and cascading associated object records from the database. Permission check prevents viewer-role users from accessing delete.
data_model_changes: Cascade delete removes the view record and all associated saved object records from the database.
implementation_notes: Delete is a cascade operation — both the view record and all its associated objects in the child table are removed. The permission model maps to the existing shared-user permission system rather than formal roles; 'viewer' refers to users granted view-only access when sharing an account.
comment_summary: Developer implemented delete with confirmation modal, cascade DB cleanup, and dropdown reset. Asked for clarification on 'viewer role' — product owner clarified it maps to the shared-user permission system where invited users may have restricted access.
